title: Cable Trace & Label
description: Identify both ends of an unlabeled cable run and document it.
safety_notes: Never unplug a live trunk/uplink to trace it without confirming impact.
Use a toner/probe or port-blink; avoid disruptive methods on production links.

steps.title: Start from the known end
steps.instruction: Identify the end you know (e.g. the switch port). Note the device, port, and any existing label. Photograph it. Ready to trace?

steps.title: Choose a non-disruptive trace method
steps.instruction: Is the link live/production? If yes, use port-blink (identify LED) or a toner on an idle pair — avoid unplugging. If the run is dead/idle, a toner/probe is fine.

steps.title: Trace live with port-blink
steps.instruction: Use the switch's port identify/blink LED (or interface flap only if approved) and follow to the patch panel/far end. Found the far end?

steps.title: Trace with toner/probe
steps.instruction: Apply the toner at the known end and probe candidate ports/panels until you find the strongest tone. Found the far end?

steps.title: Label and document both ends
steps.instruction: Apply matching labels at both ends per the site convention, photograph them, and record A-end/B-end in the inventory.

steps.title: Escalate — trace inconclusive
steps.instruction: Could not positively identify the far end without risk. Document what was checked and escalate rather than guess the mapping.
